free_text: Prompt neutron emission from U-235 fission fragments
AWRE research reactor HERALD
Thermal column
Maxwellian thermal spectrum
20 ug/cm2 evaporated deposit of U-235 on 70 microg/cm2
nickel backing.
Neutron detection in coincidence with fission
fragments.
The heights of coincident pulses from the surface
barrier counters were related to the kinetic energy of
each fragment.
The angular correlation of the prompt neutrons wit the
fragment direction is used to associate the neutrons
with the particular fragments which emitted them.
Large 250 l, 80 cm diameter, gadolinium loaded
(5% by weight) liquid NE323 scintillator counter for
neutrons. With 12 5 in diameter EMI photomultiplier
tubes type 9618B. 50% intrinsic efficiency for
detecting fast neutrons from fission events - for
gating period of 10 microsec.
Two gold-silicon surface barrier detectors for
fission fragments.
Fragments travelling within +-20 deg of the axis of the
scintillation counter, i.e. the direction of incident
neutron beam, are selected for measurement.
This work was used for energy calibration of pulse
height scales.
LF and HF average energies were determined by TOF
technique by Milton and Fraser.
Neutron detection efficiency was calculated using
measured total neutron emission and normalizing to
a value of NU of 2.43 prt/fis.
Number of neutrons emitted per fragment (Table 1)
Statistical standard deviation given

emission can arise when the steady average background
is subtracted and are of no significance.
